---
title: |
  |
  |
  | Comparative Government
  |
  |
author: |
  | Dr Chao-Yo Cheng 
  |
output:
  beamer_presentation:
    includes:
      in_header: mystyle.tex
  slidy_presentation: default
  ioslides_presentation: default
fontsize: 10pt
---

\begin{center}
\textbf{Final Essay}
\end{center}
\vspace{0.3cm}
```{r echo=FALSE, out.width="80%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("essay.pdf")
```

# Submission details \& format

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Deadline}: April 9, 2026, by 12pm (midday/noon)
  \vspace{0.3cm}
  \item \textbf{Word Count}: 1,500 words maximum (includes in-text references)
  \vspace{0.3cm}
  \item \textbf{Style}: Formal case study (no personal commentary)
  \vspace{0.3cm}
  \item \textbf{Referencing}: 
  \begin{itemize}
    \item Use a professional style (Harvard/APSA)
    \item In-text citations must include page numbers
    \item Full bibliography required
  \end{itemize}
\end{itemize}

# Core requirements

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Event Selection}: One or two locations based on events from the last 12 months
  \vspace{0.2cm}
  \item \textbf{Theoretical Application}: Address \textbf{one} specific prompt using module readings and lectures
  \vspace{0.2cm}
  \item \textbf{Sourcing}: 
  \vspace{0.1cm}
  \begin{itemize}
    \item Use news sources (e.g., \textit{Financial Times}) and thinktank reports for factual basis.
    \item Prioritize Moodle materials and academic journals.
    \item Engage thoroughly with course textbooks and lectures.
  \end{itemize}
\end{itemize}

# Questions

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{[Minorities]} Why are some states more tolerant of minorities than others?
  \vspace{0.25cm} 
  \item \textbf{[Constitutions]} Why do some constitutions protect rights better than others?
  \vspace{0.25cm}
  \item \textbf{[Authoritarianism and Democratic Backsliding]} Why do some states move toward authoritarianism while others do not?
  \vspace{0.25cm}
  \item \textbf{[Legislatures]} What is the role of legislatures in defending democracy?
  \vspace{0.25cm}
  \item \textbf{[Elections]} Why do some elections attract higher voter turnout than others?
\end{itemize}

# Exercise

\begin{itemize}
  \item Identify your event(s)
  \vspace{0.4cm}
  \item Explain how the chosen event(s) speak to one of the questions
  \vspace{0.4cm}
  \item Specify relevant weeks for your essay
\end{itemize}
